description_long: This is a circular wooden display shelf with a geometric, intersecting grid design, set atop a rectangular wooden base that features two brass-handled drawers. The circular frame encloses several open compartments, each holding decorative items such as vases, bowls, and small jars. The wood has a dark, rich finish, and the craftsmanship suggests an East Asian or traditional style, often seen in urban parks, tea houses, or upscale sidewalk displays. The drawers at the bottom provide additional storage, while the open shelving above is ideal for showcasing ornamental objects. The structure is solid and substantial, likely requiring two people to move it. The overall appearance is elegant and functional, blending both display and storage purposes.
description: A circular wooden display shelf with intersecting compartments, set on a rectangular base with two drawers and brass handles, holding various decorative items.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular frame, intersecting shelves, decorative items, and two brass-handled drawers at the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the circular frame and the alignment of the shelves, with a single decorative item visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the circular shelving and decorative items, but with less visibility of the drawer handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, highlighting the thickness of the frame and the arrangement of objects within the shelves.
